objections.description: The artifact contains only a vague 'Potential direction' statement with no concrete implementation plan, no code, no technical specification, and no acceptance criteria. The user explicitly requested a concrete implementation plan with severity-ranked concerns and acceptance criteria, but none are provided.
objections.suggested_fix: Provide a detailed technical specification including: (1) exact canonical document schema defining all required fields (issuing_organ, body_plain, etc.), (2) hydration logic or response mapping functions for each backend (PG, ES, hybrid), (3) code implementation or pseudocode for the normalization layer, (4) integration point in the benchmark harness (before grading), (5) unit tests for field mapping correctness.

objections.description: No specification of what the 'canonical document shape' should contain. The artifact mentions fields like issuing_organ and body_plain are missing from PG results but doesn't define the full canonical schema needed for grading hard_match and body_hits checks.
objections.suggested_fix: Define a complete canonical document schema listing all fields required by the grader: issuing_organ, body_plain, and any other fields used for hard_match/body_hits. Document which fields are optional vs required for each backend.

objections.description: No acceptance criteria defined. The user explicitly requested acceptance criteria but none are provided.
objections.suggested_fix: Define measurable acceptance criteria such as: (1) PG, ES, and hybrid must return identical field sets in benchmark results after normalization, (2) All required grading fields must be present before scoring, (3) Normalization must preserve original relevance ordering, (4) Performance overhead must be <Xms per query.

objections.description: The artifact proposes two alternatives (hydration vs response mapping) but doesn't analyze trade-offs or recommend a specific approach with justification.
objections.suggested_fix: Select and justify one approach: either (a) normalize per-backend response mapping at adapter layer (PGSearchAdapter, ESSearchAdapter, HybridSearchAdapter) ensuring each returns canonical shape, or (b) hydrate benchmark results post-retrieval before grading. Analyze pros/cons including maintainability, performance, and benchmark isolation.

objections.description: No details on how PGSearchAdapter will be modified to include missing fields like issuing_organ and body_plain - whether through query modification, JOINs, or fallback to original document lookup.
objections.suggested_fix: Specify the technical approach for PG field enrichment: either (1) modify PG queries to JOIN/document retrieval to include missing fields, (2) add post-processing step to hydrate from original document store, or (3) use default/placeholder values with explicit documentation of limitations.

suggestions.description: Consider whether normalization should be backend-specific (per-adapter) vs benchmark-level (pre-grading). Backend-specific normalization keeps the benchmark pure; benchmark-level normalization is easier but couples benchmark to implementation details. Recommend a position with justification.
Define testing strategy for normalization: unit tests for field mapping, integration tests to verify all backends produce equivalent output, and regression tests to catch future field omissions.
